title: Add P008 performance caps and noisy-filing safeguards
description:
## Background
Identity detectors can become noisy on large filings or filings with repeated facts. P008 needs explicit caps and suppression rules so it remains useful rather than overwhelming.

## Scope
Add limits, grouping thresholds, duplicate suppression, artifact-size safeguards, and diagnostics for filings that exceed configured bounds.

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Detector has documented caps for contexts, candidate instruments, collision groups, and artifact rows.
AC2: Duplicate fact repetition does not multiply findings.
AC3: Over-cap filings produce deterministic truncation or unsupported diagnostics with stable ordering.
AC4: Tests cover noisy/repeated facts and large fixture inputs.
AC5: Caps preserve the Microsoft positive demo without special-casing it.

## Notes
Prefer predictable conservative output over high-recall noisy output.
design: Design scope: keep P008 linear and bounded. P008 should handle large filings without walking all facts repeatedly or emitting unbounded findings.
acceptance_criteria: AC1 extraction is linear over Arelle facts; AC2 grouping and sorting are deterministic; AC3 finding inclusion respects truncation semantics; AC4 generated artifacts are bounded to registered-security rows; AC5 malformed rows produce warnings/review entries not crashes; AC6 complexity and caps are documented.

title: Create deterministic Microsoft P008 demo runbook
description:
## Background
The demo needs to be reproducible under pressure. A runbook should let someone rebuild the P008 Microsoft identity-collapse story from known inputs without relying on live services.

## Scope
Document fixture inputs, registry snapshot fixture, exact commands, expected findings, expected generated artifacts, and interpretation notes for the Microsoft 10-Q demo.

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Runbook starts from a deterministic local filing fixture and local registry snapshot fixture.
AC2: Commands produce the expected XEW-P008 finding and evidence-pack artifacts.

AC5: Runbook states that the canon provider is external and the demo consumes a snapshot.

The runbook should make the impossible-looking part obvious: XEW can flag structured identity fragility before a human notices the instruments are collapsed.

title: XEW-P009 Temporal Instrument Identity Drift for N-PORT
description:
Epic for implementing XEW-P009: Temporal Instrument Identity Drift for N-PORT filings.

Outcome:
- Produce a deterministic Bloomberg-facing demonstration that shows how XEW can detect portfolio-instrument identity fragility across a fund filing time series, not just inside one filing.
- Use N-PORT because Part C is structured, position-level, and requires investment identifiers: issuer/name/title, CUSIP if any, and at least one additional identifier such as ISIN, ticker, or another unique identifier with type.
- The demo goal is not to infer hidden corporate-action history. The goal is to prove when identity continuity is strong, when the reported identifier basis changes, and when downstream joins become fragile because continuity is only weakly evidenced.

Core P009 thesis:
- N-PORT structure reduces raw-column join drift because CUSIP, ISIN, ticker, and other identifiers live in distinct fields.
- The remaining valuable problem is temporal identity drift: the strongest available anchor for an investment changes, disappears, becomes ambiguous, or can only be bridged through a local registry snapshot.
- P009 should separate proven continuity from fragile unresolved continuity. Strong proof requires exact identifier continuity or canon/OpenFIGI registry resolution from local snapshots. Weak title/ticker/position similarity is evidence, not collapse authority.

Hard constraints:
- Fully deterministic. No LLM in the detector path.
- No live OpenFIGI, SEC, twinning, canon, HTTP, or orchestrator calls during pack-time detection.
- Provider calls are allowed only in a pre-pack registry materialization phase, following the P008 boundary.
- Evidence Pack must include exact artifacts, local registry snapshot bytes when used, generated P009 evidence JSON, deterministic hashes, and rule-basis-backed findings.
- False positives are more damaging than missed speculative cases. Ambiguity must be emitted as ambiguity.

Leverage from existing P008 and spine work:
- Reuse or generalize InstrumentRegistrySnapshot for canon/OpenFIGI-backed local identity resolution.
- Reuse P008 registry materialization and snapshot-from-canon flow where possible.
- Reuse pack history-window support for prior filings.
- Reuse generated evidence artifact pattern from generated/instrument_identity_collapse.v1.json.
- Reuse orchestrator manifest and cached S3 fetch paths for corpus assembly.
- Use br/bv/ubs for implementation planning and gates.

Suggested P009 issue-code vocabulary:
- identifier_basis_transition: same proven canonical instrument is reported under a different strongest identifier basis across time.
- strong_identifier_removed: prior CUSIP/ISIN/FIGI basis is absent in a later filing.
- registry_bridge_available: local canon/OpenFIGI snapshot proves a bridge across identifiers.
- registry_bridge_missing: a bridge would be needed but local registry has no matching row.
- registry_bridge_ambiguous: local registry maps the evidence to multiple candidate instruments.
- weak_continuity_only: continuity candidate exists only through weak keys such as title/ticker/issuer/economic facts.
- weak_key_temporal_collision: same weak key maps to multiple strong identities over the history window.

Implementation must include focused fixtures and an end-to-end proof path suitable for a demo tomorrow.

title: Parse N-PORT holdings into deterministic identity observations
description:
Implement deterministic N-PORT ingestion for P009.

Why this exists:
- Existing XEW pack flow is iXBRL/Arelle-first and currently validates forms such as 10-Q and 10-K. P009 needs NPORT-P/NPORT-P/A structured XML support.
- N-PORT Part C reports each portfolio investment with issuer/name/title, CUSIP if any, and at least one additional identifier such as ISIN, ticker, or another typed identifier. That makes it a good deterministic substrate for identity drift.

Scope:
- Add controlled support for NPORT-P and NPORT-P/A primary XML artifacts in argument validation and pack-time routing without weakening existing iXBRL behavior.
- Create a namespace-tolerant XML parser for N-PORT investment records. Use structured XML parsing, not regex.
- Extract per-holding observations with stable fields: accession, filed_date, report_period/date, series id/name/LEI when present, holding ordinal, issuer name/LEI, title or investment description, CUSIP, ISIN, ticker, other identifier type/value, asset type, issuer type, balance/units, currency, value, pct value, maturity date, coupon fields, and source element paths.
- Normalize identifiers using existing instrument_identity helpers where appropriate, but do not force P008 title grammars onto fund holdings.
- Emit parser diagnostics for missing required identity fields, duplicate holdings, unsupported identifier shape, malformed XML, and unknown schema variants.
- Preserve exact source XML bytes through the Evidence Pack artifacts.

Acceptance criteria:
- Synthetic N-PORT XML fixtures parse into byte-stable ordered observations.
- NPORT-P and NPORT-P/A are accepted only by the P009/N-PORT path and do not disturb existing Arelle iXBRL detectors.
- Parser output includes enough provenance to point a Bloomberg demo viewer from a P009 finding back to exact XML nodes.
- Parser handles the current SEC XML tech spec shape and has explicit tests for the other-identifier attribute/value variant.
- No SEC or provider network calls are made by the parser.

title: Implement XEW-P008 detector extraction and finding construction
description:
## Background
The core detector needs to show that a filing presents multiple instruments under collapsed weak identity facts. It should turn structured XBRL/iXBRL facts plus a local registry snapshot into evidence, not rely on ad hoc HTML scraping or live enrichment.

## Scope
Extract security-title, trading-symbol, exchange, CUSIP/ISIN where present, context, fact location, and local snapshot matches; group instruments by weak keys; emit findings when distinct canonical identities collapse under the same weak identity. Do not call OpenFIGI, twinning, or canon at runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Detector identifies weak-key collisions only when two or more distinct strong/canonical instrument identities are present.
AC2: Findings include the collapsed key, member instruments, fact evidence, registry-snapshot evidence, confidence inputs, and deterministic repair candidates.
AC3: Missing registry coverage degrades to a precise unsupported/missing-data issue code instead of guessing.
AC4: Detector output is stable across repeated runs on the same filing and registry snapshot.
AC5: Runtime behavior remains deterministic and offline.

## Notes
The Microsoft 10-Q case is the primary positive fixture: common stock, 3.125% notes due 2028, and 2.625% notes due 2033 all collapse under `MSFT`/`Nasdaq` weak facts.
design: Design scope: detector extracts DEI registered-security facts from Arelle, groups by context, invokes pure identity logic, and constructs DetectorFinding instances. It should be thin orchestration over tested helpers.
acceptance_criteria: AC1 detector auto-discovers as p008_identity_collapse.py; AC2 should_run skips safely without model unless upstream require-arelle is fatal; AC3 extraction recognizes Security12bTitle, TradingSymbol, SecurityExchangeName, NoTradingSymbolFlag; AC4 Microsoft-like mock facts produce one weak-key collision finding; AC5 clean fixtures produce no P008 finding; AC6 registry absent still emits collapse/review output by default.
close_reason: Implemented XEW-P008 deterministic detector, local canon/OpenFIGI snapshot consumer boundary, CLI/pack integration, generated proof artifact, docs/runbook updates, and full verification: P008 unit/E2E tests, schema/golden checks, full unittest discovery, br lint, bv --robot-insights, and UBS all passed.

title: Materialize canon OpenFIGI registries from discovered filing corpora
description:
Add a corpus-driven registry materialization phase for the XEW/OpenFIGI demo pipeline.

Why this exists:
- The right OpenFIGI registry depends on the use case and corpus. A CMBS corpus, registered public-company securities corpus, or 10-D ABS corpus will need different seed identifiers and registry versions.
- We may not know the full useful corpus until orchestrator or cached-filing discovery returns candidate filings.
- The correct boundary is not that the registry must exist before any processing. The boundary is that provider calls happen in a registry materialization phase before final pack generation, and P008 detection consumes only local snapshots.

Scope:
- Define and implement a local registry materialization workflow that takes a filing manifest or discovered candidate corpus, extracts or accepts CUSIP/ISIN/SEDOL seed identifiers, splits them by OpenFIGI id type, and runs canon registry build for each seed set.
- Support both modes: pre-known corpus seeds supplied by an operator, and discovered corpus seeds generated from cached filing artifacts during a preprocessing pass.
- Use canon OpenFIGI provider output as versioned local registry directories with _build.json provenance. Do not call OpenFIGI from cmdrvl-xew pack or detector code.
- Support incremental refresh for follow-up discoveries so new identifiers can be appended without rebuilding a million-ID registry.
- Produce a registry manifest that records corpus id, source filing manifest hash, seed files, id types, canon registry output dirs, build versions, provider options, unresolved counts, failure counts, and timestamps.
- Feed bd-2fs adapter after materialization so final P008 snapshots are derived from local canon registry output only.

Acceptance criteria:
- Given a synthetic filing manifest and synthetic extracted identifiers, the workflow writes deterministic CUSIP/ISIN/SEDOL seed files and a registry materialization manifest.
- Given a local twin or mock provider config, the workflow can run canon registry build without contacting live OpenFIGI.
- Given an operator-approved live provider config, the workflow can run canon registry build as a maintenance step before final pack generation.
- Incremental mode fetches only new identifiers already absent from the existing canon registry output.
- The final pack/demo path proves no provider calls happen during cmdrvl-xew pack.
- Tests cover pre-known seeds, discovered seeds, mixed id types, empty seed sets, provider failures, unresolved identifiers, and deterministic version naming.
- No real OpenFIGI bulk data, API keys, or generated registry output is committed.
design:
One-shot implementation design:

Module skeleton:
- src/cmdrvl_xew/openfigi_materialization.py: new local orchestration module. Own dataclasses CorpusRegistryConfig, RegistrySeedRow, RegistrySeedSet, CanonRegistryBuild, RegistryMaterializationManifest, RegistryMaterializationDiagnostic. Own functions load_filing_manifest, collect_seed_identifiers, split_seed_sets_by_id_type, write_seed_files, run_canon_registry_build, load_existing_registry_inputs, write_registry_materialization_manifest, and summarize_materialization.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/cli.py: add p008-materialize-openfigi-registry subcommand with --manifest, --seed, --seed-column, --out, --corpus-id, --version, --canon-bin, --canon-project, --provider-config, --incremental, --dry-run, --allow-live-provider, --require-twin, and --work-dir.
- tests/test_openfigi_materialization.py: unit tests with synthetic manifests, synthetic seed rows, fake canon runners, and no live OpenFIGI calls.
- docs/BLOOMBERG_OPENFIGI_XEW_DEMO.md and docs/REPRO_RUNBOOK.md: document registry materialization as a pre-pack phase.

Pipeline modes:
- Pre-known corpus: operator supplies CUSIP/ISIN/SEDOL seed CSV or JSONL files. The command normalizes, dedupes, splits by id type, and runs canon registry build.
- Discovered corpus: operator supplies a filing manifest. The command uses cached filing artifacts and early extraction outputs to collect CUSIP/ISIN/SEDOL seeds, then runs canon registry build.
- Incremental refresh: existing canon registry output is inspected first, and only new seed identifiers are sent to canon with --incremental.

Data invariants:
- I01 provider calls are allowed only in this materialization command or external canon maintenance, never inside pack or P008 detection.
- I02 seed files are sorted, deduped, id-type specific, and hash recorded.
- I03 id type is explicit: CUSIP -> ID_CUSIP, ISIN -> ID_ISIN, SEDOL -> ID_SEDOL.
- I04 live OpenFIGI requires explicit --allow-live-provider; otherwise require provider_config base_url for a twin/mock path.
- I05 API keys are never written to manifests; provider options must be sanitized the way canon sanitizes _build.json.
- I06 empty seed sets are recorded as skipped, not treated as successful registries.
- I07 materialization manifest links source corpus hash, seed hashes, canon registry dirs, _build.json hashes, unresolved counts, failures, and build versions.
- I08 the final P008 adapter consumes only local canon registry output dirs from the manifest.

Error taxonomy:
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E001 no manifest or seed input supplied.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E002 unsupported or ambiguous identifier type.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E003 no usable seed identifiers found.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E004 live provider attempted without --allow-live-provider.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E005 twin required but provider_config base_url missing.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E006 canon binary/source unavailable or version too old for openfigi provider.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E007 canon registry build failed.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E008 _build.json missing, malformed, or records only failures.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E009 incremental registry id mismatch.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E010 output path exists and overwrite policy is unclear.

Test matrix:
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U001 pre-known CUSIP seeds produce sorted deduped seed CSV and canon build command.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U002 mixed CUSIP/ISIN/SEDOL seeds split into three id-type runs.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U003 discovered filing manifest plus synthetic extracted identifiers produces expected seed sets.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U004 dry-run records planned builds without invoking canon.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U005 twin mode requires provider_config base_url and rejects live API URL by default.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U006 live mode requires --allow-live-provider and redacts api_key in logs/manifests.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U007 incremental mode excludes identifiers already present in existing canon mapping files.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-U008 provider failures and unresolved identifiers are copied into diagnostics.
- MAT-OPENFIGI-E2E001 with local twin/mock provider creates a canon registry dir, materialization manifest, and adapter-ready local output.

Verification commands:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_openfigi_materialization tests.test_p008_canon_adapter
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
- cmdrvl-xew p008-materialize-openfigi-registry --seed SEEDS_CSV --seed-column cusip --out OUT_DIR --corpus-id CORPUS --version VERSION --provider-config base_url=TWIN_URL --dry-run
- cmdrvl-xew p008-materialize-openfigi-registry --manifest MANIFEST_CSV --out OUT_DIR --corpus-id CORPUS --version VERSION --incremental --dry-run

title: Add P008 end-to-end pack and schema tests
description:
## Background
End-to-end tests need to prove the detector works through the real XEW pack flow and emits schema-valid evidence artifacts. Unit tests prove the kernel; E2E tests prove the wiring, schema, artifact generation, and verification path.

## Scope
Add fixtures and tests that run pack generation against representative filing/fixture inputs, validate findings schema, validate manifest entries, and diff golden generated artifacts.

## Required End-to-End Test Vectors
| ID | Fixture | Expected Proof |
| --- | --- | --- |
| P008-E001 | Minimal synthetic iXBRL cover-page fixture with the Microsoft-style common stock, 2028 notes, and 2033 notes facts sharing `MSFT`/`Nasdaq` weak fields | Pack emits exactly one `XEW-P008` collapse finding with three canonical members. |
| P008-E002 | Same synthetic fixture plus local registry snapshot fixture containing `BBG000BPH459`, `BBG005NPW5Z2`, and `BBG004HDR2M6` | Generated P008 artifact contains all three registry-backed identities and snapshot provenance. |
| P008-E003 | Same fixture with registry row for one instrument removed | Pack still completes, emits missing-registry diagnostic for the absent row, and does not invent FIGI data. |
| P008-E004 | Fixture with repeated duplicate facts for one instrument | Pack suppresses duplicate noise and keeps the same single collapse finding/member set. |
| P008-E005 | Fixture with one malformed/unsupported instrument title | Pack emits an explicit unsupported-title diagnostic and excludes that candidate from canonical collision membership. |
| P008-E006 | Same fixture and registry snapshot run twice from a clean temp output directory | Findings, generated artifact, manifest entry, and content hashes are byte-identical. |
| P008-E007 | Fixture with no identity collapse, only one instrument under a weak key | Pack emits no P008 collapse finding and still validates successfully. |
| P008-E008 | Pack run without the required local registry snapshot when P008 is enabled in strict mode | CLI exits with deterministic actionable error; no live OpenFIGI/canon/twinning call is attempted. |

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: E2E pack test produces an XEW-P008 finding for the Microsoft-style identity-collapse fixture.
AC2: Generated findings validate against `xew_findings.schema.v1.json`.
AC3: Generated artifacts validate against the documented Evidence Pack contract.
AC4: Re-running the same test produces byte-identical generated artifacts.
AC5: Tests implement every vector P008-E001 through P008-E008.
AC6: Tests use local synthetic fixtures and local registry snapshot fixtures only.

## Notes
Keep fixtures minimized but realistic enough to demonstrate the actual SEC/iXBRL evidence path. The real Microsoft filing belongs in the demo runbook, not as a committed E2E artifact.
design: Design scope: end-to-end tests prove P008 survives the real pack/verify path using tiny synthetic artifacts, not real SEC artifacts or live services.

title: P009 golden fixtures, unit tests, and end-to-end proof pack
description:
Build the test suite that proves P009 works and protects against false positives.

Why this exists:
- A Bloomberg demo must be defensible. The detector needs tests that prove both positive detections and refusal to overclaim.

- Ambiguous registry snapshot: emits registry_bridge_ambiguous with sorted candidates.

title: Implement P008 registry snapshot consumer boundary
description:
## Background
XEW should not implement the canon/OpenFIGI registry provider. P008 only needs a deterministic, offline consumer boundary: given a local registry snapshot produced elsewhere, validate it and use it for identity-collapse evidence.

## Scope
Implement the XEW-side snapshot reader, schema validation, lookup APIs, provenance handling, missing-entry behavior, and deterministic duplicate-handling rules. Do not build OpenFIGI ingestion, twinning client generation, canon registry writes, or provider refresh jobs in this repository.

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: XEW reads a documented local registry snapshot format with version, generated-at, source, and rows.
AC2: Lookups support the fields P008 needs: CUSIP, ISIN, ticker/exchange, normalized title, FIGI, composite FIGI, share-class FIGI, and canonical signature where available.
AC3: Missing, duplicate, stale, and malformed snapshot entries produce explicit structured diagnostics.
AC4: Tests prove lookup ordering is stable and no live network, OpenFIGI, twinning, or canon-provider code path is called.
AC5: The bead explicitly points provider implementation to canon as external prerequisite/context, not XEW scope.

## Notes
Canon should own the OpenFIGI registry provider and snapshot generation once twinning supports the OpenFIGI OpenAPI cleanly. XEW consumes the resulting snapshot.

title: Package the one-command Bloomberg XEW demo path
description:
Create the actual operator-facing demo path for Bloomberg: one command or one short runbook that starts from cached SEC filing bytes and ends with a verified, readable Evidence Pack showing P008 instrument identity collapse.

Why this exists:
- Infrastructure tasks are necessary, but the meeting needs a clean demonstration with a crisp input, deterministic processing, and a clear output.
- The demo should make the impossible-looking part obvious: from SEC filing bytes alone, XEW finds registered instruments collapsed under weak ticker/exchange facts, then enriches from a local FIGI-backed registry snapshot without any live lookup during pack generation.

Scope:
- Add a demo script or runbook command sequence that orchestrates: S3 artifact source, Arelle taxonomy bootstrap/check, pack generation, pack verification, and focused P008 output assertions.
- Use the MSFT accession 0001193125-26-191507 as the canonical demo case unless a stronger case is found.
- Produce concise console output suitable for a live meeting: input accession, source S3 keys, Arelle version/config, P008 members, registry status, pack path, and verification result.
- Include a dry-run/check mode that validates prerequisites without downloading large objects.
- Keep this inside the XEW repo as reproducibility/demo documentation and scripts only; do not build hosted orchestration or alert delivery here.

Acceptance criteria:
- A fresh operator can run the demo with documented AWS profile/env vars and get either a clear skip/prereq failure or a verified Evidence Pack.
- The final output explicitly lists common stock, 3.125% Notes due 2028, and 2.625% Notes due 2033 for MSFT.
- When a registry snapshot is supplied, the output highlights resolved distinct registry identities; when absent and not required, the output says snapshot_absent clearly.
- The demo command never calls SEC or OpenFIGI live.
- The demo docs include a 60-second talk track explaining why ticker/CUSIP-style weak identity is not enough and why OpenFIGI/canon makes the repair deterministic.
design:
One-shot implementation design:

Module skeleton:
- scripts/run_bloomberg_p008_demo.sh: final operator wrapper. It should call the lower-level flatten-s3, arelle install/check, adapter, pack, and verify commands. It must support --dry-run, --aws-profile, --taxonomy-home, --work-dir, --canon-registry, --p008-snapshot, --source-layout extracted|xbrl|auto, and --keep-work.
- docs/BLOOMBERG_OPENFIGI_XEW_DEMO.md: meeting runbook and talk track. This is a demo document, not general product docs.
- README.md and docs/REPRO_RUNBOOK.md: short pointers to the demo path after the standalone runbook exists.
- No hosted service, browser UI, or LLM integration in this bead.

Demo narrative contract:
- Step 1: Show the filing accession and cached S3 source, proving the input is normal SEC filing bytes already in our cache.
- Step 2: Show Arelle offline parsing, proving this is real XBRL/iXBRL semantics rather than HTML scraping.
- Step 3: Show P008 weak identity collapse: Common stock, 3.125% Notes due 2028, and 2.625% Notes due 2033 share weak MSFT/Nasdaq identity facts.
- Step 4: Show canon/OpenFIGI snapshot enrichment from local files, proving FIGI-backed identities can repair the collapse without live lookup during detection.
- Step 5: Show verify-pack success and the snapshot copied into artifacts/p008_registry_snapshot.json, proving the output is reproducible and tamper-evident.

Console output requirements:
- accession, CIK, filed date, source layout, and source S3 URI.
- Arelle version, resolution mode, and taxonomy config home.
- canon source/binary check, registry id/version, build provenance hash, and snapshot id.
- P008 weak key, member count, member titles, extraction source, registry statuses, and FIGI fields when resolved.
- pack path and verify-pack result.
- explicit no_live_sec and no_live_openfigi indicators.

Demo preflight checks:
- AWS CLI available.
- AWS profile can head selected S3 keys.
- Arelle import and taxonomy package registry available or installable from the S3 bundle.
- cmdrvl-xew exposes flatten-s3, p008-snapshot-from-canon, pack, and verify-pack.

- Output directory policy is clear before writing.

Acceptance criteria:
- --dry-run completes in under 30 seconds without downloading large filing objects.
- A normal run produces one verified Evidence Pack and prints a concise summary table for the three MSFT instruments.
- The run can use an already materialized P008 snapshot or a canon registry directory plus adapter.
- If the registry path is omitted, demo output falls back to snapshot_absent mode and says exactly what proof is missing.
- The runbook includes a 60-second talk track, a 5-minute technical drilldown, and a fallback path for missing AWS credentials.
- The script exits nonzero on partial pack generation, missing registry when required, or verify-pack failure.

Verification commands:

- scripts/run_bloomberg_p008_demo.sh --aws-profile salt_profile --canon-registry CANON_REGISTRY_DIR --work-dir WORK_DIR

title: Implement pure P008 identity model and deterministic title grammar

## Background
The detector must not depend on fragile string heuristics scattered through extraction code. A pure identity model gives P008 stable, unit-testable behavior for instrument titles, rates, maturities, par values, exchanges, tickers, CUSIPs, ISINs, and FIGIs.

AC4: Unit tests prove stable ordering, stable serialization, and no network or LLM dependency.

This bead unblocks extraction and registry matching while staying independent enough to test exhaustively.

title: Adapt canon OpenFIGI registry output into P008 snapshots
description:
Implement a deterministic adapter that converts a canon OpenFIGI registry build output directory into the local P008 snapshot consumed by --p008-registry-snapshot.

Why this exists:
- canon now owns OpenFIGI provider calls and materializes standard registry files.
- XEW already owns the P008 snapshot consumer and must remain offline during detection.
- The Bloomberg demo needs a believable bridge from a canon registry build to an Evidence Pack, without hand-authored FIGI JSON.

Inputs:
- A canon registry directory produced by registry build --source openfigi.
- registry.json for registry id, version, source, materialized time, seed count, and resolved count.
- Root mapping files such as cusip-to-figi.json, cusip-to-ticker.json, cusip-to-name.json, and corresponding ISIN or SEDOL variants.
- Optional _build.json for provider, id type, seed hash, seed column, unresolved rows, failures, ambiguous rows, API call counts, and sanitized provider options.
- Optional XEW extracted P008 instrument identities so the adapter can attach canonical_signature and normalized_title without guessing from provider text.

Scope:
- Add a CLI or library function that reads canon registry output with structured JSON parsing and emits cmdrvl.canon.openfigi_registry_snapshot JSON.
- Preserve provenance in snapshot source metadata: producer canon, dataset openfigi, registry id, registry version, seed column, seed hash, OpenFIGI id type, build file hash, and source directory hash or path.
- Map rows only by exact identifiers and deterministic fields. Prefer CUSIP or ISIN. Use canonical_signature only when supplied by XEW extracted identities. Never infer a FIGI from ticker plus name alone.
- Deduplicate identical rows deterministically and surface true ambiguity as snapshot rows that P008 reports ambiguous.
- Support the MSFT demo path where the filing itself may not expose CUSIP or ISIN for every registered instrument by allowing an explicit seed-to-instrument overlay generated ahead of the demo and recorded in provenance.
- Refuse malformed canon registries, unsupported canonical types, missing figi mappings, and provider failure-only builds with clear diagnostics.

Acceptance criteria:
- Given a synthetic canon OpenFIGI registry fixture, the adapter emits a valid P008 snapshot that InstrumentRegistrySnapshot.load accepts.
- Fixture tests cover resolved mapping, missing mapping, duplicate-identical rows, multiple FIGI ambiguity, malformed registry.json, malformed _build.json, and provider failures recorded in _build.json.
- Adapter output is byte-stable for the same inputs, including sorted rows and sorted metadata keys.
- The adapter performs no network calls and never shells out to canon during pack generation.
- The generated snapshot can be passed to cmdrvl-xew pack with --p008-require-registry and produces resolved or explicitly ambiguous P008 registry statuses.
- Docs show the maintenance-time sequence: build a seed corpus, run canon registry build, run the adapter, then run cmdrvl-xew pack from local files only.
design:
One-shot implementation design:

Module skeleton:
- src/cmdrvl_xew/p008_canon_adapter.py: new adapter module. Own dataclasses CanonRegistryMetadata, CanonBuildProvenance, CanonMappingEntry, P008SnapshotRowDraft, CanonAdapterReport. Own functions load_canon_registry, load_build_provenance, collect_mapping_files, adapt_openfigi_registry, write_p008_snapshot, and stable_json_bytes.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/cli.py: add subcommand p008-snapshot-from-canon with flags --canon-registry, --out, --snapshot-id, --instrument-overlay, --generated-at, --require-build-provenance, and --allow-provider-failures. The command only reads local files and writes one JSON snapshot.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/instrument_registry.py: avoid behavior changes unless needed for metadata validation. The existing loader remains the pack-time consumer boundary.
- docs/REPRO_RUNBOOK.md and README.md: add the maintenance-time sequence and offline pack-time sequence.
- tests/test_p008_canon_adapter.py: adapter unit and CLI tests with synthetic canon registry directories only.

Data invariants:
- I01 local-only: adapter never imports HTTP clients, never shells out to canon, and never calls OpenFIGI.
- I02 structured parse: every canon JSON file is parsed as JSON, never transformed with string replacement.
- I03 exact identity first: rows are built from exact CUSIP or ISIN mappings when available.
- I04 no weak inference: ticker plus name can enrich an already matched row but can never create the FIGI match.
- I05 deterministic output: rows sort by figi, cusip, isin, canonical_signature, normalized_title, ticker, exchange.
- I06 provenance preserved: snapshot source includes canon registry id/version/source, seed column/hash/count, build hash, OpenFIGI id type, and source directory reference.
- I07 ambiguity preserved: if one instrument key maps to multiple distinct FIGIs, emit distinct rows so P008 reports ambiguous rather than choosing.
- I08 provider failures visible: _build.json failures and unresolved rows are copied into adapter report metadata or diagnostics.

Error taxonomy:
- P008-CANON-E001 registry path missing or not a directory.
- P008-CANON-E002 missing or malformed registry.json.
- P008-CANON-E003 registry source is not openfigi or registry id is not openfigi-prefixed.
- P008-CANON-E004 no supported figi mapping file found.
- P008-CANON-E005 mapping entry missing input, canonical_id, canonical_type, or rule_id.
- P008-CANON-E006 unsupported canonical_type for FIGI mapping.
- P008-CANON-E007 malformed or missing _build.json when --require-build-provenance is set.
- P008-CANON-E008 provider failures present and --allow-provider-failures is not set.
- P008-CANON-E009 instrument overlay missing required identifier or canonical_signature fields.
- P008-CANON-E010 output snapshot fails InstrumentRegistrySnapshot.load validation.

Test matrix:
- P008-CANON-U001 loads minimal openfigi-cusip registry and emits one valid snapshot row.
- P008-CANON-U002 merges cusip-to-figi, cusip-to-ticker, and cusip-to-name for the same input.
- P008-CANON-U003 preserves _build.json provenance including seed hash, seed column, id type, unresolved count, and failure count.
- P008-CANON-U004 refuses missing registry.json.
- P008-CANON-U005 refuses non-openfigi registry source.
- P008-CANON-U006 preserves multiple distinct FIGI rows for one identifier and P008 reports ambiguous.
- P008-CANON-U007 dedupes duplicate-identical rows byte-stably.
- P008-CANON-U008 overlay attaches canonical_signature and normalized_title only when identifier matches exactly.
- P008-CANON-U009 refuses ticker/name-only overlay matches.
- P008-CANON-E2E001 adapter output feeds cmdrvl-xew pack with --p008-require-registry using the existing MSFT P008 synthetic fixture.

Verification commands:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_p008_canon_adapter
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_p008_identity_registry tests.test_p008_detector tests.test_p008_pack_e2e
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
- cmdrvl-xew p008-snapshot-from-canon --canon-registry REGISTRY_DIR --out SNAPSHOT_JSON --snapshot-id SNAPSHOT_ID --instrument-overlay OVERLAY_JSON

title: Bloomberg P009 evidence-pack runbook and narrative
description:
Write the demo runbook and narrative for the P009 Bloomberg conversation.

Why this exists:
- The technical detector is only useful in the meeting if the evidence pack tells a clear story: structured filings still leave temporal identity fragility, and OpenFIGI/canon can deterministically bridge it.
- Bloomberg should see an outcome, not a pile of JSON.

Scope:
- Write a short runbook showing the full deterministic chain: orchestrator filing selection, cached S3 artifacts, N-PORT XML parsing, seed extraction, canon/OpenFIGI registry materialization, P009 pack generation, verify-pack, and summary.
- Include a demo script that highlights the strongest visual: a timeline where a fund holding moves from CUSIP/ISIN identity to a different basis, and the local FIGI bridge proves or fails continuity.
- Include an explanation of why this is different from simply calling OpenFIGI on one CUSIP: the value is time-series corpus processing, reproducible evidence, ambiguity handling, and no runtime provider dependency.
- Include the exact artifacts to show: xew_findings.json, generated/nport_identity_drift.v1.json, copied registry snapshot artifact, pack_manifest.json hash lines, and verify-pack output.
- Include a fallback demo path using synthetic fixtures if live corpus selection or registry materialization is blocked.

Acceptance criteria:
- A future operator can run the demo from the runbook without asking for hidden chat context.
- The narrative uses P009 terminology consistently and does not use the word demo in command or skill names unless unavoidable in docs prose.
- The runbook makes clear where provider calls are allowed and where they are forbidden.
- The runbook includes one paragraph on why OpenFIGI is better than ticker/CUSIP alone in this workflow: global, persistent, multi-level instrument identity that can connect local identifiers across contexts.

title: XEW-P008: Instrument Identity Collapse detector
description:
## Background
XEW-P008 should make identity collapse visible when a filing uses weak security facts that make distinct instruments look identical. The flagship Bloomberg demo is a deterministic Microsoft 10-Q case where common stock and multiple note securities collapse under weak ticker/exchange facts while OpenFIGI-capable registry evidence separates them.

## Scope
Deliver the XEW detector, schema/docs, local registry snapshot consumer boundary, evidence-pack artifacts, tests, runbook, and Bloomberg proof artifact. Exclude canon provider implementation, OpenFIGI API ingestion, twinning generation, and any live-network enrichment from XEW scope.

## Success Criteria
SC1: XEW emits schema-valid P008 findings and generated artifacts for a reproducible instrument identity collapse fixture.
SC2: The implementation is deterministic, offline-capable, and independent of LLMs or live OpenFIGI calls.
SC3: The detector can consume a canon/OpenFIGI snapshot contract produced externally by canon.
SC4: Tests cover unit, registry-boundary, pack, schema, golden artifact, performance, and determinism cases.
SC5: The final demo package explains the XEW + canon + OpenFIGI outcome without placing canon provider work in the XEW repo.

## Notes
This epic is intentionally ambitious but bounded: XEW proves the detector and artifact surface; canon produces the registry snapshot in a separate workstream.
design: Epic design: P008 should make Bloomberg see OpenFIGI as an identity-repair layer for public machine-readable disclosure, not as a lookup API. XEW owns deterministic detection and Evidence Packs; canon owns local OpenFIGI registry snapshots; AI is optional after proof generation.
acceptance_criteria: AC1 all child beads are closed; AC2 P008 detects Microsoft-style weak ticker/exchange collapse deterministically; AC3 P008 works without live network and without LLM; AC4 local registry fixture resolves the three Microsoft instruments; AC5 generated artifacts are manifest-hashed; AC6 docs/demo package clearly explain the Bloomberg outcome.

title: Generate P008 corpus manifests from orchestrator filing lists
description:
Add an optional intake path that asks the CMD+RVL orchestrator for a list of filings and writes a local corpus manifest for the P008 scanner.

Why this exists:
- The orchestrator can already answer natural-language filing-list questions such as recent filings for a ticker or a described time series.
- For the Bloomberg demo, this lets us move from one hand-picked accession to a deterministic shortlist built from a queryable filing universe.
- The scanner and pack generation should remain offline and cached-artifact based after the manifest is written.

Scope:
- Add a script or command that sends an orchestrator query through the cmdrvl-cli orchestrator endpoint and normalizes the response into the p008-scan-corpus manifest schema.
- Capture query text, tenant, timestamp, cmdrvl-cli version when available, raw response hash, and normalization diagnostics in manifest provenance.
- Support both recent-filings queries and ticker/time-series queries where the orchestrator returns CIK, accession, filing date, form, ticker, issuer, and source description.
- Validate that each returned row has CIK, accession, filed_date, and form before it is eligible for scanning.
- Allow manual review/edit of the manifest before scanning. XEW scanning consumes the manifest file only and never re-queries orchestrator.

Acceptance criteria:
- Given a synthetic orchestrator JSON response fixture, the intake emits a stable CSV or JSONL manifest accepted by bd-348.
- Invalid or incomplete rows are retained in a diagnostics sidecar and excluded from scan-ready rows.
- The manifest records enough provenance to reproduce the exact orchestrator question and response hash.
- The command supports a dry-run that prints the orchestrator question and expected output path without sending the query.
- Documentation makes the boundary explicit: orchestrator may discover filing lists, but P008 detection and pack generation do not call orchestrator, SEC, OpenFIGI, or LLMs.
- No real orchestrator responses, filing payloads, or generated packs are committed.
design:
One-shot implementation design:

Module skeleton:
- src/cmdrvl_xew/orchestrator_manifest.py: new local intake module. Own dataclasses OrchestratorQueryConfig, OrchestratorRawResponse, FilingManifestRow, FilingManifestProvenance, FilingManifestDiagnostic. Own functions build_orchestrator_question, run_orchestrator_query, parse_orchestrator_json_envelope, normalize_filing_rows, write_manifest_csv, write_manifest_jsonl, and write_manifest_provenance.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/cli.py: add p008-manifest-from-orchestrator subcommand with --query, --tenant, --out, --format csv|jsonl, --raw-out, --provenance-out, --cmdrvl-project, --timeout, --dry-run, and --allow-partial.
- tests/test_orchestrator_manifest.py: unit tests using synthetic cmdrvl JSON envelope fixtures and no real orchestrator calls.
- docs/BLOOMBERG_OPENFIGI_XEW_DEMO.md and docs/REPRO_RUNBOOK.md: document orchestrator as optional manifest discovery before deterministic scan/pack.

Command boundary:
- Default cmdrvl project path should be /Users/zac/Source/cmdrvl/cmdrvl-cli when present, with an override flag/env var for other checkouts.
- Invoke cmdrvl as: uv run --project CMDRVL_CLI_PROJECT cmdrvl --json orchestrator query --tenant TENANT QUERY.
- Parse .data.content from the standard JSON envelope. If the orchestrator was instructed to return JSON, decode .data.content as a JSON string and validate the row schema.
- Never put global --json at the end of the command; it belongs immediately after cmdrvl.
- The command can use --timeout when supported by cmdrvl-cli, but tests should not require a live orchestrator.

Suggested query contract:
- Ask orchestrator to return only JSON with top-level filings array.
- Each filing object should include cik, accession, filed_date, form, ticker, issuer_name, description, and source.
- Example query: Return recent 10-K and 10-Q filings for companies likely to have multiple registered securities, with CIK, accession, filed_date, form, ticker, issuer_name, and a short reason. Return only JSON.

Data invariants:
- I01 manifest rows must include CIK, accession, filed_date, and form to be scan-ready.
- I02 accession is normalized to dashed accession form and original value is preserved in diagnostics when changed.
- I03 filed_date is normalized to YYYY-MM-DD and also date_partition YYYYMMDD.
- I04 incomplete rows are excluded from the scan-ready manifest but retained in diagnostics.
- I05 raw response bytes are hashed and optionally stored outside the repo for audit.
- I06 manifest output is sorted by filed_date descending, then ticker, then accession unless the user asks to preserve orchestrator order.
- I07 this intake may query orchestrator, but p008-scan-corpus and pack must consume local manifest files only.

Error taxonomy:
- ORCH-MAN-E001 cmdrvl-cli project missing.

title: Add P008 quality gate and determinism hardening
description:
## Background
P008 is valuable only if it can be trusted as a deterministic detector. Quality gates should verify no hidden dependence on wall-clock time, random ordering, live services, fuzzy matching, or LLM output.

## Scope
Add or update project quality gates for determinism, schema validation, fixture stability, sorting, no-network behavior, no-provider-runtime behavior, and explicit ambiguous-case refusal for P008.

## Required Gate Checks
| ID | Gate | Expected Proof |
| --- | --- | --- |
| P008-G001 | Unit suite | All P008-U001 through P008-U016 vectors pass. |
| P008-G002 | E2E suite | All P008-E001 through P008-E008 vectors pass. |
| P008-G003 | Schema validation | P008 findings validate against `xew_findings.schema.v1.json`. |
| P008-G004 | Evidence Pack verification | Generated P008 artifacts and manifest entries pass `verify-pack`/schema checks. |
| P008-G005 | Determinism rerun | Clean-output rerun produces byte-identical findings/artifacts/hashes. |
| P008-G006 | No network/provider runtime | Tests fail if P008 runtime attempts OpenFIGI, EDGAR, canon provider, twinning, HTTP, or model calls. |
| P008-G007 | No fuzzy matching | Tests fail if ambiguous candidates are auto-selected or scored into a guessed FIGI. |
| P008-G008 | Performance/noise caps | Noisy/repeated-fact fixture remains within documented caps and emits stable truncation diagnostics if caps are exceeded. |

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Quality gate runs unit, pack, schema, and golden artifact tests relevant to P008.
AC2: Repeated runs produce identical findings and generated artifacts.
AC3: Tests fail on accidental live-network usage or missing local registry snapshot fixtures.
AC4: Documentation lists the exact commands required before closing the epic.
AC5: Existing XEW quality gates remain intact.
AC6: Gate checks implement P008-G001 through P008-G008.

## Notes
This bead is the guardrail that keeps the demo from becoming a one-off trick and keeps canon provider work outside XEW runtime.
design: Design scope: final hardening bead for deterministic trust. This bead should catch shortcuts introduced by implementation agents.
acceptance_criteria: AC1 full unittest suite passes; AC2 schema validation passes for P008 findings; AC3 verify-pack passes on P008 fixture; AC4 determinism rerun comparison passes; AC5 no P008 network/OpenFIGI/EDGAR/canon/twinning access; AC6 no P008 LLM/model imports; AC7 no fuzzy matching; AC8 ambiguous registry candidates never select a FIGI; AC9 gate checks implement P008-G001 through P008-G008.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic P008 runbook/quality gate surface and verified no-network, schema, golden, noise-cap, full unittest, br lint, bv --robot-insights, and UBS gates.

title: Add deterministic P008 corpus scanner for demo candidate selection
description:
Add a deterministic batch scanner that can evaluate a known accession corpus for P008 instrument identity collapse candidates and produce a ranked demo shortlist.

Why this exists:
- A single MSFT demo case is valuable, but Bloomberg will care more if we can show the same deterministic machinery finding a queue of fragile filings from a known corpus.
- The scanner makes the demo less cherry-picked and helps select stronger cases with registered instruments that can be enriched by canon/OpenFIGI snapshots.
- This remains an offline cached-artifact workflow. It does not call SEC, OpenFIGI, an LLM, or ask-orchestrator during scanning.

Inputs:
- A CSV or JSONL corpus manifest with at least ticker or issuer label, CIK, accession, filed_date, form, and optional source_layout.
- Cached S3 artifacts resolved through bd-oor.
- Optional P008 snapshots produced through bd-2fs.

Scope:
- Add a p008-scan-corpus command or script that reads the manifest, runs the cached S3 plus Arelle path for each accession, and extracts only the P008 summary needed for ranking.
- Support bounded runs with --max-filings, --fail-fast, --continue-on-error, --source-layout, --aws-profile, --taxonomy-home, and --work-dir.
- Emit stable JSONL and CSV summaries with accession, issuer, form, filed_date, P008 finding count, collapse group count, max member count, member titles, registry status counts, source extraction mode, and pack path when retained.
- Keep generated packs optional. Default should retain only summaries and logs unless --keep-packs is set.
- Include deterministic ranking: highest resolved/ambiguous collapse member count first, then more distinct instrument kinds, then newest filed date, then accession.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests cover manifest parsing, ranking, summary extraction from P008 generated artifacts, skip/error rows, and deterministic output ordering.
- An integration script can scan a tiny two-row synthetic manifest without AWS.
- An optional real-S3 run can scan a small operator-supplied manifest and skip cleanly when AWS credentials are unavailable.
- The final output can be used by the Bloomberg demo runbook to choose MSFT or a stronger candidate without changing detector logic.
- No live provider calls or real filing payloads are committed.
design:
One-shot implementation design:

Module skeleton:
- src/cmdrvl_xew/p008_corpus_scan.py: new batch scanner module. Own dataclasses CorpusRow, ScanConfig, P008ScanSummary, P008ScanError, RankedCandidate. Own functions load_corpus_manifest, run_one_candidate, extract_p008_summary, rank_candidates, write_scan_jsonl, and write_scan_csv.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/cli.py: add p008-scan-corpus subcommand with --manifest, --out, --work-dir, --aws-profile, --taxonomy-home, --source-layout, --registry-snapshot-dir, --max-filings, --keep-packs, --dry-run, --continue-on-error, and --fail-fast.
- scripts/run_p008_corpus_scan_demo.sh: thin operator helper for an optional Bloomberg stretch demo.
- tests/test_p008_corpus_scan.py: unit tests with synthetic manifests and synthetic generated P008 artifacts.

Ranking contract:
- Rank 1: candidates with registry resolved or ambiguous rows before snapshot_absent rows.
- Rank 2: larger max collapse member count before smaller groups.
- Rank 3: more distinct instrument kinds before fewer kinds.
- Rank 4: newer filed_date before older.
- Rank 5: accession lexical order for stable ties.

Non-negotiables:
- No live SEC, OpenFIGI, or ask-orchestrator calls during scanning.
- Scanner can invoke local cmdrvl-xew functions or subprocess commands, but every invocation must be recorded in a per-row log.
- A failed filing row must never poison the ranking for successful rows unless --fail-fast is set.
- Generated packs are deleted or placed in work-dir according to --keep-packs, never committed.

Test matrix:
- SCAN-U001 parses CSV manifest with accession, CIK, filed_date, form, ticker, and source_layout.
- SCAN-U002 parses JSONL manifest and normalizes accession/date.
- SCAN-U003 extracts P008 summary from generated instrument_identity_collapse.v1.json.
- SCAN-U004 ranks candidates deterministically across tie-breakers.
- SCAN-U005 records skip rows for missing S3 source and continues when configured.
- SCAN-U006 dry-run validates manifest and planned S3 keys without pack generation.
- SCAN-U007 optional registry snapshot directory maps accessions to local snapshots.
- SCAN-E2E001 tiny synthetic two-row scan produces stable JSONL and CSV outputs without AWS.

Verification commands:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_p008_corpus_scan
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
- cmdrvl-xew p008-scan-corpus --manifest MANIFEST_CSV --out OUT_DIR --dry-run
- scripts/run_p008_corpus_scan_demo.sh --manifest MANIFEST_CSV --aws-profile salt_profile --max-filings 5 --dry-run

- This preserves the deterministic scan boundary: orchestrator can discover a candidate filing list before the scan, but p008-scan-corpus itself does not call orchestrator.

title: Update XEW docs and roadmap for P008 positioning

## Background
P008 needs clear positioning in XEW docs: it detects instrument identity collapse in filings and consumes external registry snapshots, while canon owns snapshot production.

## Scope
Update README/roadmap/evidence-pack docs to describe P008, its deterministic constraints, its relationship to canon/OpenFIGI, and its Bloomberg demo value.

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Docs explain what Instrument Identity Collapse is and why weak ticker/exchange identity is fragile.
AC2: Docs describe XEW as the detector/evidence layer and canon as the registry snapshot producer.

title: Implement complete-submission SGML .nc artifact extractor
description:
Implement deterministic extraction from edgar-data-full xbrl cache objects so cmdrvl-xew can use complete-submission .nc artifacts as a filing source.

Why this exists:
- The S3 xbrl cache does not use accession directories. It stores flat objects at xbrl/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION.nc.
- Inspection of MSFT 0001193125-26-191507 showed complete-submission SGML with submission metadata, document records, the primary iXBRL HTML, and attached XBRL artifacts.
- The S3-backed source should not contain ad hoc parsing logic. It should call a tested extractor with stable artifact outputs.

Scope:
- Parse SEC complete-submission SGML document records using explicit state-machine or structured token logic, not brittle whole-file regex replacement.
- Extract submission metadata: accession number, filing date, form type, public document count, sequence, document type, filename, description, and text length when available.
- Preserve exact document text bytes when materializing artifacts, except for a documented optional unwrap of the outer XBRL marker around inline XBRL document text when present.
- Classify primary HTML, schema, calculation, definition, label, presentation, instance, and other attachments into the same flat or typed artifact shape accepted by flatten and pack.
- Reject path traversal, duplicate unsafe filenames, empty primary document candidates, accession mismatches, and unexpected binary/compressed payloads with clear diagnostics.
- Record original S3 object provenance separately from extracted document provenance: bucket, key, etag, last_modified, content_length, and extraction hash.
- Keep real .nc filing payloads out of the repo. Use tiny synthetic SGML fixtures for unit tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Synthetic .nc tests cover primary iXBRL, EX-101.SCH, linkbase attachments, non-XBRL attachments, malformed document boundaries, duplicate filenames, and missing primary document.
- The MSFT object xbrl/20260429/0001193125-26-191507.nc can be extracted locally into artifacts that pack accepts with --require-arelle and offline_only taxonomy resolution.
- Extractor output is deterministic for file ordering, output paths, normalized metadata, and hashes.
- The S3 artifact source can choose extracted layout, xbrl .nc layout, or auto without duplicating parser logic.
- No live SEC calls are required.
design:
One-shot implementation design:

Module skeleton:
- src/cmdrvl_xew/sgml_submission.py: new parser and materializer module. Own dataclasses SubmissionMetadata, SubmissionDocument, SgmlExtractionResult, SgmlExtractionDiagnostic. Own functions parse_complete_submission, classify_submission_documents, materialize_submission_artifacts, safe_submission_filename, and stable_submission_manifest.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/flatten.py: keep current local directory behavior. Add a small reusable entry point only if needed to flatten materialized .nc output after sgml_submission produces an EDGAR-like directory.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/s3_source.py: not implemented in this bead, but bd-oor must call sgml_submission instead of duplicating parser logic.
- tests/test_sgml_submission.py: synthetic complete-submission fixtures only.
- docs/REPRO_RUNBOOK.md: document that xbrl/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION.nc is a complete-submission cache object, not an accession directory.

Parser contracts:
- I01 byte preservation: document text bytes are preserved exactly unless the extractor is explicitly unwrapping only the outer XBRL marker.
- I02 deterministic parse: parser is a line-oriented state machine over submission/document/text sections, not a whole-file regex rewrite.
- I03 safe output paths: filenames are normalized to a safe relative basename or typed subpath; path traversal and absolute paths are refused.
- I04 accession consistency: requested accession, submission accession, and output provenance must agree when all are present.
- I05 primary selection: primary iXBRL/HTML is selected by document type, sequence, and filename in deterministic priority order.
- I06 attachment classification: schema and linkbase files are classified by document type and extension, with unknown attachments preserved but not selected as primary.
- I07 collision handling: duplicate output filenames never overwrite. Identical bytes may be deduped with metadata, distinct bytes must raise a collision diagnostic.
- I08 bounded memory: parser supports streaming or chunked reading for large .nc objects and enforces configurable max bytes for synthetic tests.
- I09 provenance: output records source object identity, extracted document metadata, sha256, byte count, sequence, document type, filename, and output relative path.

Error taxonomy:
- SGML-E001 input file missing or empty.
- SGML-E002 submission header missing required accession or filing date.
- SGML-E003 malformed document boundary or unterminated text section.
- SGML-E004 requested accession does not match submission accession.
- SGML-E005 unsafe filename or path traversal.
- SGML-E006 duplicate filename with different bytes.
- SGML-E007 no primary iXBRL/HTML candidate.
- SGML-E008 unsupported binary or compressed payload inside a document selected for parsing.
- SGML-E009 output directory collision or unsafe overwrite.
- SGML-E010 extraction manifest validation failure.

Test matrix:
- SGML-U001 parses minimal complete submission metadata and one primary HTML document.
- SGML-U002 extracts primary iXBRL wrapped in the outer XBRL marker and preserves inner HTML bytes.
- SGML-U003 extracts EX-101.SCH plus CAL, DEF, LAB, and PRE linkbases into stable output paths.
- SGML-U004 preserves non-XBRL attachments as other artifacts and excludes them from primary selection.
- SGML-U005 rejects missing primary document.
- SGML-U006 rejects unsafe filenames such as parent traversal and absolute paths.
- SGML-U007 detects duplicate filename collision with different bytes.
- SGML-U008 accepts duplicate filename only when bytes are identical and records dedupe metadata.
- SGML-U009 rejects accession mismatch.
- SGML-U010 emits deterministic manifest JSON for permuted document ordering only when semantic order is identical.
- SGML-E2E001 extracts the MSFT xbrl/20260429/0001193125-26-191507.nc object staged locally and feeds pack with --require-arelle offline_only.

Verification commands:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_sgml_submission
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_flatten_forms tests.test_p005_taxonomy tests.test_p008_detector
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests

title: Production-grade Arelle and S3 artifact ingestion for XEW
description:

title: P009 rule basis, semantics, and false-positive contract
description:
Define the P009 rule basis and output semantics before code changes.

Why this exists:
- P009 must be impressive without becoming a fuzzy entity-resolution engine.
- N-PORT has structured identifier fields, so the detector must not pretend that every weak similarity is a proven join.
- The public demo should show strict evidence classes: proven continuity, registry-bridged continuity, ambiguous bridge, missing bridge, and weak continuity only.

Scope:
- Add the XEW-P009 pattern to the issue-code/rule-basis specs with citations to the SEC Form N-PORT Part C identifier requirements and the current EDGAR Form N-PORT XML technical specification.
- Define the deterministic identity-basis precedence used by P009: FIGI from local registry, CUSIP, ISIN, other typed identifier, ticker, normalized title/issuer/economic facts.

title: Define XEW-facing OpenFIGI registry snapshot fixture contract
description:
## Background
The canon/OpenFIGI provider belongs outside XEW, but P008 needs a precise fixture contract so implementation and demos can proceed deterministically before the provider is complete.

## Scope

AC4: XEW tests can use the fixture without depending on live OpenFIGI or canon internals.
AC5: Notes identify the separate canon provider bead as the owner of snapshot production.

## Notes
This is a boundary contract bead. It exists to keep XEW deterministic while leaving provider construction in canon.

title: Add comprehensive P008 unit tests
description:
## Background
The risk in identity collapse detection is silent false confidence. Unit tests should aggressively cover canonicalization, weak-key grouping, registry lookup edge cases, deterministic repair rules, and explicit refusal to guess.

## Scope
Add focused tests for the pure identity module, registry snapshot consumer, grouping logic, error taxonomy, sorting, unsupported cases, and no-network/no-LLM invariants.

## Required Unit Test Vectors
| ID | Layer | Input / Fixture | Expected Proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| P008-U001 | Title grammar | `Common stock, $0.00000625 par value per share` with ticker `MSFT`, exchange `Nasdaq` | Parses as common stock with par value `0.00000625`, equity kind, stable normalized title, and no debt fields. |
| P008-U002 | Title grammar | `3.125% Notes due 2028` with ticker `MSFT`, exchange `Nasdaq` | Parses as debt note with coupon `3.125`, maturity year `2028`, stable normalized title, and no equity par field. |
| P008-U003 | Title grammar | `2.625% Notes due 2033` with ticker `MSFT`, exchange `Nasdaq` | Parses as debt note with coupon `2.625`, maturity year `2033`, stable normalized title, and no equity par field. |
| P008-U004 | Canonical signature | The three Microsoft instruments from U001-U003 | Produces three distinct canonical signatures even though ticker/exchange are identical. |
| P008-U005 | Weak-key grouping | Three Microsoft instruments with weak key `{ticker: MSFT, exchange: Nasdaq}` | Emits one collapse group with exactly three members, sorted deterministically by canonical signature. |
| P008-U006 | Negative grouping | One Microsoft instrument repeated across multiple facts/contexts | Emits no collapse finding and records duplicate/repetition suppression deterministically. |
| P008-U007 | Registry lookup | Snapshot row for common stock FIGI `BBG000BPH459` plus composite/share-class FIGIs | Resolves common stock to the exact row and preserves FIGI/composite/share-class provenance fields. |
| P008-U008 | Registry lookup | Snapshot row for 2028 notes FIGI `BBG005NPW5Z2` | Resolves the 2028 note by strong title/signature and does not confuse it with common stock. |
| P008-U009 | Registry lookup | Snapshot row for 2033 notes FIGI `BBG004HDR2M6` | Resolves the 2033 note by strong title/signature and does not confuse it with 2028 notes. |
| P008-U010 | Duplicate registry rows | Two byte-identical rows for the same FIGI/signature | Deduplicates or reports duplicate-identical provenance without multiplying candidates or findings. |
| P008-U011 | Ambiguous registry rows | Two conflicting rows for the same weak key/signature | Emits an ambiguous-registry diagnostic and never selects a FIGI. |
| P008-U012 | Missing registry row | Filing instrument has no matching local snapshot row | Emits missing-registry diagnostic and never invents or live-fetches a FIGI. |
| P008-U013 | Strong-key precedence | Instrument has CUSIP/ISIN plus noisy title/ticker fields | Exact strong identifier match wins; weak ticker/exchange alone cannot override it. |
| P008-U014 | Unsupported grammar | `Notes` or `Debt Securities` without rate/maturity/identifier detail | Returns explicit unsupported/ambiguous-title error and does not create a canonical instrument identity. |
| P008-U015 | Determinism guard | Same members and registry rows in randomized input order, run twice | Output ordering and serialized JSON bytes are identical across runs. |
| P008-U016 | Runtime boundary | Test monkeypatch forbids network/model modules and live clocks | Identity/registry modules do not import or call OpenFIGI, EDGAR, LLM/model clients, random, or wall-clock APIs. |

## Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Tests implement every vector P008-U001 through P008-U016.
AC2: Registry states cover resolved, unresolved, ambiguous, invalid, absent, and duplicate-identical rows.
AC3: Collision tests include MSFT common stock plus the two note securities and prove identical weak key but distinct canonical identities.
AC4: Ambiguous candidates never select a FIGI or silently downgrade severity.
AC5: Stable ordering and byte-stable serialization are asserted directly.
AC6: Identity modules do not import network, EDGAR, OpenFIGI, LLM/model, random, or wall-clock modules.

## Notes
These tests should make future twinning/provider changes safer by pinning the local snapshot contract while keeping provider implementation outside XEW.
design: Design scope: tests should protect the deterministic kernel first, then detector orchestration. Unit tests must make it hard for later agents to add fuzzy matching, network calls, or silent ambiguous selection.
acceptance_criteria: AC1 tests implement P008-U001 through P008-U016 exactly; AC2 registry states cover resolved/unresolved/ambiguous/invalid/absent/duplicate-identical; AC3 title parser tests cover supported and unsupported grammar; AC4 collision tests include MSFT common stock plus two notes; AC5 ambiguous candidates do not select a FIGI; AC6 identity modules do not import network, EDGAR, OpenFIGI, LLM/model, random, or wall-clock modules; AC7 repeated runs assert byte-stable serialization.
close_reason: Implemented XEW-P008 deterministic detector, local canon/OpenFIGI snapshot consumer boundary, CLI/pack integration, generated proof artifact, docs/runbook updates, and full verification: P008 unit/E2E tests, schema/golden checks, full unittest discovery, br lint, bv --robot-insights, and UBS all passed.

title: Upgrade and pin Arelle runtime for XEW
description:
Upgrade cmdrvl-xew to use a current, pinned Arelle runtime and prove detector compatibility.

Current state:
- Local runtime observed with uv is arelle-release 2.38.7.
- PyPI latest observed on 2026-06-09 is arelle-release 2.41.4.
- pyproject.toml currently has no runtime dependency on arelle-release; Arelle is optional/imported dynamically.

Scope:
- Decide whether cmdrvl-xew should pin arelle-release as a normal dependency, an optional extra such as [arelle], or a documented production prerequisite. Favor deterministic production behavior without surprising lightweight installs.
- Upgrade to the chosen current Arelle release and update lock/config/docs as appropriate for this repo.
- Run the full unit suite and a real cached-filing smoke test against the MSFT 10-Q accession 0001193125-26-191507.
- Audit Arelle API breakage in loader setup, PackageManager initialization, taxonomy package registry loading, and detector use of model facts/contexts/relationships.
- Clean up any warnings caused by changed Arelle or lxml behavior when practical, especially truth-testing FutureWarnings in detectors.

Acceptance criteria:
- cmdrvl-xew doctor reports the upgraded Arelle version.
- A cached MSFT pack succeeds with --require-arelle --resolution-mode offline_only after installing the existing 2018-2026 S3 taxonomy bundle.
- P008 evidence for MSFT comes from source.extraction == arelle, not inline_html fallback.
- Unit tests cover version detection/toolchain recording and Arelle-required failure behavior.
- README/docs clearly state the supported Arelle install path and why production should use --require-arelle.

Verification commands expected:
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
- cmdrvl-xew doctor --arelle-xdg-config-home <taxonomy-home>
- cmdrvl-xew pack ... --require-arelle --resolution-mode offline_only --arelle-xdg-config-home <taxonomy-home>
- cmdrvl-xew verify-pack --pack <pack-dir>
design:
One-shot implementation design:

Module skeleton:
- pyproject.toml and uv.lock: choose and record deterministic Arelle install strategy. Preferred shape is an optional extra such as arelle = [arelle-release==2.41.4] so lightweight installs stay light but production/demo installs are pinned.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/toolchain.py: ensure reported Arelle version comes from the actual imported runtime.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/doctor.py: report Arelle import status, version, taxonomy package registry status, and whether offline_only packs have enough configured taxonomy packages.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/pack.py: audit Arelle import, controller setup, PackageManager initialization, offline package loading, and model fact extraction under the upgraded runtime.
- src/cmdrvl_xew/arelle_setup.py: verify taxonomy package installation remains compatible with the upgraded Arelle release.
- tests/test_arelle_runtime.py or tests/test_arelle_setup.py: add focused tests for version detection, require-arelle failure, and taxonomy config path behavior.

Runtime invariants:
- I01 --require-arelle fails before pack output if Arelle import or model load fails.
- I02 --resolution-mode offline_only performs no network taxonomy fetches.
- I03 --no-arelle remains a debug/test path and never appears in production demo docs.
- I04 doctor is read-only and never installs or mutates taxonomy packages.
- I05 Arelle version, resolution mode, and taxonomy config home are recorded in toolchain metadata.
- I06 P008 evidence for MSFT must come from source.extraction == arelle in the real demo path.

Test matrix:
- ARELLE-U001 doctor reports imported Arelle version when arelle-release is installed.
- ARELLE-U002 doctor reports a clear missing-Arelle diagnostic when not installed.
- ARELLE-U003 --require-arelle refuses when import is unavailable.
- ARELLE-U004 taxonomy package registry path is read from --arelle-xdg-config-home.
- ARELLE-U005 install-packages handles the existing 2018-2026 S3 taxonomy bundle.
- ARELLE-U006 pack records the upgraded Arelle version in toolchain metadata.
- ARELLE-E2E001 MSFT 0001193125-26-191507 packs with --require-arelle and --resolution-mode offline_only after installing the S3 taxonomy bundle.

Verification commands:
- uv pip install -e .[arelle,jsonschema]
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest tests.test_arelle_setup
- PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
- cmdrvl-xew doctor --arelle-xdg-config-home TAXONOMY_HOME
- cmdrvl-xew arelle install-packages --bundle-uri TAXONOMY_BUNDLE --aws-profile salt_profile --arelle-xdg-config-home TAXONOMY_HOME
- cmdrvl-xew pack --require-arelle --resolution-mode offline_only --arelle-xdg-config-home TAXONOMY_HOME ...

title: Add cached-S3 Arelle P008 end-to-end demo regression
description:
Create a deterministic end-to-end regression/demo path proving cached S3 filing artifacts plus Arelle plus P008 work together for the Bloomberg instrument-identity-collapse story.

Reference demo case:
- Ticker: MSFT.
- CIK: 0000789019.
- Accession: 0001193125-26-191507.
- Filed date: 2026-04-29, date partition 20260429.
- Extracted source: s3://edgar-data-full/extracted/20260429/0001193125-26-191507/.
- Primary iXBRL observed: 10-Q/msft-20260331.htm.
- P008 should identify a weak ticker/exchange collapse across common stock, 3.125% Notes due 2028, and 2.625% Notes due 2033.

Scope:
- Provide a script or documented command sequence that starts from S3 cached artifacts and ends with a verified Evidence Pack.
- Install/bootstrap the existing S3 Arelle taxonomy bundle when needed: s3://edgar-data-full/xew/arelle-taxonomy-packages/start=2018/end=2026/sha256=a650dbc7f9d63ea03a051c1f589d77fdd76c254e2a8ee5faa522617c34362ca8/xew-arelle-taxonomy-packages-2018-2026-20260203.tgz.
- Run pack with --require-arelle and --resolution-mode offline_only.
- Assert P008 evidence source is arelle and that the three expected instruments are present.
- Once canon/OpenFIGI snapshots exist, extend the same test path to assert registry enrichment without live OpenFIGI calls.

Acceptance criteria:
- The e2e path skips clearly when AWS credentials/profile or the S3 bucket are unavailable.
- When enabled, it produces a verified Evidence Pack with a P008 finding.
- The output check confirms source.extraction == arelle for P008 member facts.
- The output check confirms registry status is snapshot_absent until a snapshot is explicitly provided, never a live lookup.
- Docs explain how this demonstrates deterministic filing-byte to instrument-identity-collapse detection.

Add the operational path that makes P009 usable by the CMD+RVL skill and demo workflow.

Why this exists:

Scope:
- Add or extend a P009 command group that can consume a filing manifest from orchestrator and process a time series for one fund/series.
- Support cached S3 materialization using the existing fetch-s3 paths, including extracted and xbrl layouts where applicable.
- Produce corpus seed files for canon/OpenFIGI from discovered N-PORT CUSIP/ISIN/other identifier observations.
- Allow a pre-pack registry materialization step to run canon when explicitly requested, with the same live-provider safety posture as P008.
- Run pack with current accession plus deterministic history arguments and optional P009 registry snapshot.
- Run verify-pack and emit a compact machine-readable summary that the cmdrvl-gtm skill can present.
- Keep the skill implementation itself out of this repo, but document the intended command surface for cmdrvl-gtm/skills/xew-identity-fragility or a future renamed P009 skill.

Acceptance criteria:

title: Define P008 canon OpenFIGI registry handoff for the Bloomberg demo
description:
Define the exact handoff between canon materialized OpenFIGI registries and XEW P008 registry snapshots.

Why this exists:
- canon source is now at v0.6.1 / 07eaf41 and implements the OpenFIGI provider in src/registry/provider.rs.
- canon registry build snapshots OpenFIGI into local registry files plus _build.json provenance. Normal lookup after that is local.
- XEW must not call OpenFIGI, canon, twinning, HTTP, or an LLM during pack generation.
- For the Bloomberg demo, the Evidence Pack should show both the weak ticker/exchange collapse and the deterministic repair path from a local canon/OpenFIGI registry artifact.

Scope:
- Document the local P008 snapshot contract that cmdrvl-xew consumes through --p008-registry-snapshot.
- Define how a canon registry build output directory maps into that contract: registry.json, root mapping files, and _build.json provenance.
- Treat canon as the upstream producer. XEW owns only the consumer contract, diagnostics, adapter tests, and demo validation.
- Use the canon command shape: canon registry build --source openfigi --seed SEED --seed-column COLUMN --output DIR --version VER with optional provider config such as id_type=ID_CUSIP, api_key, and base_url for twins.
- Require the demo runbook to check the actual installed canon binary or build from the pulled canon repo, because the current installed binary reports 0.3.0 while the pulled source is 0.6.1.
- Make the deterministic boundary visible in pack output and docs: provider calls happen only during registry maintenance, never during XEW detection.

Acceptance criteria:
- The docs describe a complete local-file handoff from canon OpenFIGI registry output to XEW P008 snapshot input.
- The snapshot schema covers figi, ticker, exchange, security title, normalized title, canonical signature, CUSIP, ISIN, composite FIGI, share-class FIGI, market sector, security type, and name.
- The contract preserves canon registry id, registry version, build provenance, seed hash, seed column, OpenFIGI id type, and source path or hash.
- P008 pack generation with --p008-require-registry still fails clearly when no snapshot is supplied.
- Tests cover snapshot_absent, resolved, missing, ambiguous, duplicate_identical, and snapshot_invalid.
- No live OpenFIGI calls are introduced into cmdrvl-xew.
design:
One-shot contract design:

Files to update:
- docs/XEW_EVIDENCE_PACK_CONTRACT_V1.MD: keep the authoritative P008 snapshot schema and add canon registry output provenance fields.
- README.md: replace stale future-provider language with canon v0.6.1 provider reality and the local snapshot boundary.
- docs/REPRO_RUNBOOK.md: add the canonical maintenance-time handoff sequence.
- tests/test_p008_identity_registry.py, tests/test_p008_detector.py, and tests/test_p008_pack_e2e.py: strengthen contract tests if any snapshot status or provenance field is missing.

Snapshot contract additions to document:
- source.provider = canon.
- source.dataset = openfigi.
- source.canon_registry_id and source.canon_registry_version.
- source.canon_build_sha256 or source.canon_build_path.
- source.seed_column, source.seed_sha256, and source.openfigi_id_type when known.
- rows include figi plus optional composite_figi and share_class_figi. The current P008 loader requires figi and can consume the optional fields.

Boundary rules:
- XEW detection only reads the snapshot file copied into artifacts/p008_registry_snapshot.json.
- XEW detection never calls canon, OpenFIGI, twinning, HTTP, or LLM/model runtimes.
- Missing, ambiguous, duplicate-identical, invalid, and absent snapshots are diagnostic states, not excuses to guess.
- Registry metadata is provenance, not lookup behavior.

Contract test checklist:
- snapshot_absent without --p008-registry-snapshot.
- strict failure with --p008-require-registry and no snapshot.
- resolved when one row matches by CUSIP, ISIN, canonical_signature, or normalized title plus compatible weak facts.
- ambiguous when multiple distinct FIGIs match.
- duplicate_identical when duplicate rows are byte-equivalent after normalization.
- snapshot_invalid when JSON is malformed or figi is invalid.
- pack manifest includes artifacts/p008_registry_snapshot.json when supplied.
