title: Characterize and support edgar-data-full xbrl cache objects
description:
Understand the sibling S3 xbrl/ cache and make it usable as an XEW filing source when it provides better/fuller artifacts than extracted/.

Observed S3 facts from 2026-06-09 investigation:
- extracted layout for MSFT: s3://edgar-data-full/extracted/20260429/0001193125-26-191507/ has typed directories such as 10-Q/ and EX-101.SCH/.
- xbrl layout is different: date partition with flat objects, e.g. s3://edgar-data-full/xbrl/20260429/0001193125-26-191507.nc exists.
- The MSFT .nc object is about 31,304,208 bytes, ContentType binary/octet-stream, and appears likely to contain a fuller accession/submission payload than the extracted typed artifact subset.
- Exact prefix xbrl/20260429/0001193125-26-191507/ is empty; code must not assume accession directories under xbrl/.

Scope:
- Download and inspect representative .nc objects without committing them. Determine whether they are SGML complete submission text, an XBRL bundle, compressed data, or another internal encoding.
- Define deterministic extraction rules from xbrl/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION.nc into the same flat artifact shape expected by cmdrvl-xew pack.
- Compare extracted/ vs xbrl/ coverage for MSFT 0001193125-26-191507 and at least one other accession with CAL/DEF/LAB/PRE files.
- Decide fallback order: prefer extracted/ typed artifacts when sufficient, prefer xbrl/ .nc when it contains fuller canonical source, or expose an explicit source preference.
- Ensure no live SEC calls are required for this path.

Acceptance criteria:
- A documented S3 key resolver can locate both extracted/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION/ and xbrl/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION.nc.
- Tests cover the xbrl/ flat .nc layout, missing object behavior, wrong date behavior, and accession normalization.

- The MSFT accession can be resolved through this path or a clear diagnostic explains why extracted/ remains the supported source for now.

- This bead does not add live SEC fetching.

title: Add S3-backed EDGAR artifact source for cmdrvl-xew
description:
Add first-class S3 input support so cmdrvl-xew can consume cached EDGAR artifacts without operators manually copying prefixes to local temp directories.

Current state:
- pack and flatten use local pathlib paths only.
- The known working manual path is: ask-orchestrator provides cik/accession/filed_date, aws s3 cp stages extracted/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION/ locally, cmdrvl-xew flatten normalizes it, then cmdrvl-xew pack generates the Evidence Pack.
- This should become an explicit, deterministic CLI/library workflow.

Scope:
- Add an S3 source abstraction that can list/head/download required objects using an AWS profile or ambient AWS credentials.
- Support at minimum s3://edgar-data-full/extracted/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION/ typed EDGAR directories.
- Integrate the xbrl/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION.nc findings from bd-mwx once that cache shape is characterized.
- Materialize only needed artifacts into a deterministic local cache/work directory for Arelle and hashing; Arelle can keep receiving local paths.
- Record S3 provenance in Evidence Pack metadata: bucket, key, etag, last_modified, content_length, selected source layout, and original S3 URI.
- Expose ergonomic CLI flags. Candidate shape: cmdrvl-xew flatten-s3 or cmdrvl-xew fetch-s3, with --s3-uri or bucket/date/accession flags, --aws-profile, --source-layout extracted|xbrl|auto, --out, and --force.
- Keep SEC fetch separate; S3 cache ingestion must not require XEW_USER_AGENT unless it falls back to live HTTP, which should not happen by default.

Acceptance criteria:
- MSFT 0001193125-26-191507 can be flattened from s3://edgar-data-full/extracted/20260429/0001193125-26-191507/ using --aws-profile salt_profile or equivalent test configuration.
- The resulting flat directory can feed pack with --require-arelle --resolution-mode offline_only.
- Unit tests cover URI parsing, AWS profile option plumbing, object selection, provenance recording, empty prefix diagnostics, and unsupported layouts.
- Integration/e2e test script is included but skips cleanly when AWS credentials/profile are unavailable.
- README and repro runbook document the no-live-SEC path: orchestrator filing date plus S3 cached artifacts plus Arelle taxonomy bundle.
- No real filing payloads are committed.

- src/cmdrvl_xew/cli.py: add flatten-s3 subcommand with flags --s3-uri, --bucket, --date, --accession, --source-layout extracted|xbrl|auto, --aws-profile, --out, --force, and --dry-run. The command emits a local flat artifact directory compatible with pack.

Implementation constraints:
- Use AWS CLI subprocess calls as the first implementation path, consistent with arelle_setup.py and the empty dependency list in pyproject.toml.
- Isolate all subprocess calls in s3_source.py so tests can provide fake runners without network or AWS credentials.
- Support explicit URI mode and structured mode. URI mode accepts s3://edgar-data-full/extracted/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION/ or s3://edgar-data-full/xbrl/YYYYMMDD/ACCESSION.nc. Structured mode composes the URI from bucket, date, accession, and source-layout.
- Dry-run performs head/list checks and prints selected objects and estimated bytes without downloading.
- Materialization always writes into an operator-provided output directory and refuses unsafe overwrites unless --force is set.
- Evidence Pack provenance should include S3 bucket, key, etag, last_modified, content_length, selected layout, local materialization path, and sha256 after download.

Error taxonomy:
- S3SRC-E001 malformed S3 URI.
- S3SRC-E002 AWS CLI missing.
- S3SRC-E003 AWS command failed.
- S3SRC-E004 extracted prefix empty.
- S3SRC-E005 xbrl .nc object missing.
- S3SRC-E006 ambiguous auto layout selection.
- S3SRC-E007 unsupported source layout.
- S3SRC-E008 output path exists and --force was not set.
- S3SRC-E009 downloaded object sha256 or byte count mismatch.
- S3SRC-E010 SGML extraction failed for xbrl layout.

Test matrix:
- S3SRC-U001 parses extracted prefix URI and normalizes trailing slash.
- S3SRC-U002 parses xbrl object URI and validates .nc suffix.
- S3SRC-U003 composes URIs from bucket, date, accession, and layout.
- S3SRC-U004 list-prefix empty returns S3SRC-E004.
- S3SRC-U005 head-object missing returns S3SRC-E005.
- S3SRC-U006 dry-run prints selected objects and makes no copy calls.
- S3SRC-U007 extracted layout downloads typed objects and calls existing flatten.
- S3SRC-U008 xbrl layout downloads one .nc object and calls sgml_submission materializer.
- S3SRC-U009 auto prefers extracted when sufficient or xbrl when extracted is absent, with a deterministic diagnostic.
- S3SRC-U010 provenance JSON is stable and includes etag, last_modified, content_length, selected layout, and original URI.
- S3SRC-E2E001 with AWS_PROFILE=salt_profile can flatten MSFT 0001193125-26-191507 from extracted/20260429.
- S3SRC-E2E002 with AWS_PROFILE=salt_profile can flatten MSFT 0001193125-26-191507 from xbrl/20260429 after bd-3c5.

title: Build P009 temporal identity ledger and alias graph
description:
Build the deterministic temporal model that P009 detection runs over.

Why this exists:
- A single N-PORT filing can say what identifiers a holding reported. P009 needs to compare a time series and classify how identity anchors change.
- The graph must be strict enough to impress Bloomberg without generating fuzzy false positives.

Scope:
- Create a P009 holding identity observation model and a dated ledger sorted by report period, accession, series, holding ordinal, and source path.
- Assign each observation a strongest_reported_basis using deterministic precedence: local-registry FIGI when resolved, CUSIP, ISIN, typed other identifier, ticker, weak descriptive basis.
- Build alias graph nodes for exact identifiers and local-registry FIGI rows. Edges are allowed only for exact same identifier, canon/OpenFIGI snapshot mapping, or same observation carrying multiple identifiers.
- Build weak evidence groups from issuer/name/title/ticker/economic facts only as candidate continuity evidence. Weak groups must never create canonical instrument nodes by themselves.
- Detect graph events: basis transition, strong identifier disappearance, bridge available, bridge missing, bridge ambiguous, weak-only continuity, and temporal weak-key collision.
- Include caps and deterministic truncation for very large funds so output remains reproducible.

Acceptance criteria:
- Given synthetic observations over three periods, the ledger and alias graph output is byte-stable.
- Exact CUSIP continuity creates a proven chain.
- CUSIP to FIGI via local registry creates a registry-bridged chain.
- Same title/ticker/value without an exact bridge produces weak_continuity_only and not a resolved chain.
- Same weak key mapping to two strong identities produces weak_key_temporal_collision.
- Ambiguous registry rows preserve candidates and do not choose a winner.
